description_long: This hammer features a robust, metallic head with a flat striking face and a slightly angular, reinforced back, suggesting it is designed for heavy-duty use. The head is attached to a long, straight handle that appears to be constructed from a composite or metal material, segmented with textured grip sections for enhanced handling. The handle is relatively slender and features three distinct grip-enhancing segments, likely for shock absorption and improved control. The head is solid and blocky, with no visible claw or peen, indicating it is a sledge-type hammer suitable for demolition or driving stakes. The overall color scheme is a combination of dark gray and metallic silver, with a small red accent near the head. The surface of the hammer is hard and matte, with no visible signs of wear or branding.
description: A heavy-duty sledge-type hammer with a solid metallic head and a segmented, textured handle in dark gray and silver tones.
description_view_0: Front view shows the flat striking face of the hammer head and the straight, segmented handle extending downward.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the thickness of the hammer head, the angular back, and the three grip-enhancing segments along the handle.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the reinforced back of the hammer head and the alignment of the handle segments.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the same features as view 1, with a clear look at the red accent near the head.
attribute_handle_type: Straight composite handle with three textured, segmented grip sections for enhanced control and shock absorption.
attribute_head_type: Solid sledge-type head with a flat striking face and a reinforced, angular back; no claw or peen present.
